title: Cracking the Code of Ancient Egypt
story: A French soldier stumbled upon a mysterious stone that would change history forever. In 1799, Captain Pierre-François Bouchard discovered the Rosetta Stone in an Egyptian village. This ancient artifact held the key to understanding hieroglyphics, the long-lost writing system of ancient Egypt. Found on July 15, the stone featured the same text in three different scripts, allowing scholars to finally decode the secrets of the pharaohs.
activity_question: What ancient writing system did the Rosetta Stone help decode?
follow_up_question: Why was having the same text in three scripts so important for decoding hieroglyphics?

title: Red Planet Revealed: Mars Up Close
story: Cameras clicked in space as Mariner 4 zoomed past Mars. On July 15, 1965, this robotic explorer beamed back the first close-up pictures of the Red Planet. Scientists eagerly awaited these images, hoping to see alien cities or lush forests. Instead, they discovered a cratered, desert-like world. While not what they expected, these photos opened a new chapter in our understanding of our cosmic neighbor.

title: Jim Thorpe's Olympic Triumph and Redemption
story: Jim Thorpe soared to victory in 1912, dominating the Olympic decathlon in Stockholm. The incredible athlete placed in the top four in all ten events, setting a record with 8,413 points and clinching the gold medal. Sadly, his triumph was short-lived. On July 15, 1913, officials stripped Thorpe of his medal for playing professional baseball. But justice prevailed. Decades later, in 1982, the Olympic committee finally restored his rightful gold, recognizing Thorpe's extraordinary achievement.

title: Penicillin's Frozen Journey to Save Lives
story: Two scientists made a chilly discovery that would change medicine forever. On July 15, 1941, Howard Florey and Norman Heatley presented freeze-dried mold cultures of penicillin. This cold method helped preserve the life-saving antibiotic, making it easier to transport and use. Their work built on earlier penicillin research, paving the way for this 'miracle drug' to help countless people fight infections.
activity_question: What did the scientists present on July 15, 1941?
bonus_fact: Penicillin was first discovered by accident when mold contaminated a petri dish in a lab.

title: Henry Johnson: A True American Hero
story: Bravery knows no bounds! Henry Johnson, born in Tallahassee, Florida in 1892, became a legendary soldier. On July 15, he entered the world, destined for greatness. title: Elton John's Rocket Ride to the Top
story: Elton John's music blasted off like a rocket in 1972! His album 'Honky Chateau' zoomed to the top of the charts on July 15, becoming his first ever No. 1 in America. The record included the spacey hit 'Rocket Man', which had fans singing along to its catchy tune about a lonely astronaut.

title: Mother Cabrini: A Saint's Journey Begins
story: A tiny baby girl named Frances Xavier Cabrini was born in Italy in 1850. Little did anyone know she would grow up to become Mother Cabrini, the first American saint!
